finding: The ordering of task-id lists and path lists inside audit output is never pinned: §5.6 tasks array per file, §5.4 (tasks: id1, id2) annotation, and §3.3 comma-separated paths all derive from the §5.2 task-to-file mapping, but no rule states whether ids/paths appear in task-file order, git-show order, or alphabetical. Two testers implementing §19.3 TestAudit_FullShape must each pick an order, producing contradictory golden assertions for the mainline multi-task-per-file case.
location: §5.6 affected_files JSON schema (also §5.4 file entry format, §3.3 task_acceptance expected_evidence)

resolved.evidence: Round-8 revision applied to spec/0.23.0.md §5.2: task-id and file-path list ordering pinned to alphabetical; §5.4/§5.6/§3.3 inherit, extending the §3.2 determinism contract to the full prompt body

suggestion: Add one sentence to §5.2: task ids within a file task list, and file paths within a task mapped-file list, are sorted alphabetically; §5.4 annotations, §5.6 tasks, and §3.3 paths inherit this order.
